title: BKSY: International Defense Demand and 2026 Guidance Analysis
text: Key Takeaways: BlackSky's fair value estimate has been revised upward to $27.63 per share, driven by strong international defense demand and concrete 2026 revenue guidance of $120-$145 million. International Defense Contracts: The company secured multiple significant international agreements: An eight figure international contract for a Gen 3 sovereign space based intelligence solution, Multiple seven-figure contracts from new international defense customers for time-sensitive intelligence services, A seven-figure extension with an existing international customer. 2026 Outlook: Analysts view the 2026 guidance as conservative regarding a potential recovery in domestic volumes, suggesting possible upside if U.S. customer demand performs better than projected. However, some note that slower-than-expected domestic volume recovery remains a key risk. Valuation Adjustments: Notable changes include: Fair value increased slightly from $27.29 to $27.63, Revenue growth assumption decreased from 30.09% to 23.99%, Net profit margin improved from 7.21% to 7.70%. Market Position: Analysts describe BlackSky as a secondary beneficiary of increased defense spending rather than a primary driver, with price targets clustered between $23-$27.
